member.name: Justin Madders
member.role: MP for Ellesmere Port and Bromborough
member.synopsis: Justin Madders is the Labour MP for Ellesmere Port and Bromborough , and has been an MP continually since 7 May 2015.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, when he expects the RSS backlog to be resolved.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence (MOD) continues to work closely with the Armed Forces Pension Scheme (AFPS) administrator to issue Remediable Service Statements (RSS) and reduce outstanding volumes as quickly and accurately as possible. To date, this has included...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking to resolve backlogs in the issuing of RSS to veterans.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence (MOD) continues to work closely with the Armed Forces Pension Scheme (AFPS) administrator to issue Remediable Service Statements (RSS) and reduce outstanding volumes as quickly and accurately as possible. To date, this has included...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Batteries: EU Law
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment her Department has made of the potential risk of Great Britain becoming a market for lower-specification or less sustainable batteries if regulatory alignment with EU R
writtenQuestions.answerText: No assessment has been made of the potential risk of Great Britain becoming a market for lower-specification or less sustainable batteries as a result of divergence with EU Regulation 2023/1542. While some obligations have already come into force in EU Re...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Batteries: EU Law
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment her Department has made of the potential requirement for businesses that manufacture electrical products to produce separate Northern Ireland-compliant variants if EU R
writtenQuestions.answerText: The EU Batteries Regulation applies directly in Northern Ireland under the Windsor Framework. We will shortly publish guidance for businesses in Northern Ireland to help them comply with these new requirements. Through stakeholder engagement, we understan...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Batteries: EU Law
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether her Department plans to establish transitional arrangements to allow businesses to sell through existing stock in the event that requirements pursuant to EU Regulation 2023/154
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra is working with the Devolved Governments to review the UK’s producer responsibility legislation for batteries. As part of this review, my officials held a series of workshops with industry earlier this year to explore the impacts of regulatory consi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Batteries: EU Law
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether she plans to a) introduce requirements in Great Britain equivalent to EU Regulation 2023/1542 on batteries; b) what the indicative timeline is for any such introduction; c) and
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra is working with the Devolved Governments to review the UK’s producer responsibility legislation for batteries. As part of this review, my officials held a series of workshops with industry earlier this year to explore the impacts of regulatory consi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Suicide: Mental Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to implement the suicide prevention strategy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to delivering the five-year Suicide Prevention Strategy for England. The strategy sets out over 100 actions across the Government and wider organisations, all aimed at saving lives, providing early intervention, prevention init...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: British Industrial Competitiveness Scheme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the British Industrial Competitive Scheme on levels of business investment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: From 2027, The British Industrial Competitiveness Scheme will reduce electricity costs by up to £40 per megawatt hour for over 10,000 eligible manufacturing businesses. This will help bring electricity costs more in line with other European economies and ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Manufacturing Industries: Capital Investment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps he is taking to support manufacturers to increase capital intensity.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Last year this government published our Modern Industrial Strategy, setting out our long-term approach to strengthening domestic capability. The complimentary Advanced Manufacturing Sector Plan outlines both sector-specific and cross-cutting measures desi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Lord Mandelson and Morgan McSweeney
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, pursuant to the answer of 23rd June to question 8158 on the Lord Mandelson and Morgan McSweeney, if he can confirm whether these documents have been withheld at the request of the Metropolitan Police.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the Government’s statement and release of information on 1st June, providing an update on the response to the Humble Address. The documents published in the first and second tranches contain the entirety of the documents the Gove...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Transport: Motor Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what percentage of cars rented by her department are a) electric and b) UK made and what steps she is taking to encourage departmental travel conducted in cars manufactured in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Refineries: UK Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential economic impact of delaying the inclusion of the refineries sector in a CBAM on the sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government recognises the role that refineries play in energy security and the UK’s industrial base. The Government published a call for evidence (https://www.gov.uk/government/calls-for-evidence/future-of-the-uk-downstream-oil-sector/future-of-the-uk...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Refineries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to support the refineries sector while oil supplies are disrupted due to military conflict in the Middle East.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the critical role UK refineries are playing in ensuring our fuel security. The Government continues to monitor the evolving situation in the Middle East closely, but there is no indication that current global developments are aff...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Motorways: Lighting
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps is her department taking to ensure National Highways includes the views of local residents in stakeholder consultations when deciding to remove lights over motorways.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department expects National Highways to carry out appropriate consultation where changes to the strategic road network may impact local communities. Where a proposal to remove lighting would directly affect a local community, National Highways would s...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Transport: Electric Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her department encourages officials to rent electric vehicles when hiring vehicles for official business.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government’s policy is that all official travel must be efficient and cost-effective. The Department does encourage the use of electric vehicles for vehicle hire. However, short-term vehicle hires can happen at short notice and are subject to vehicle ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Eating Disorders: Ellesmere Port and Bromborough
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the availability of specialist eating disorder support for patients in Ellesmere Port and Bromborough.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department is working closely with NHS England to strengthen community-based eating disorder (ED) services and to improve outcomes and recovery for patients, including in Ellesmere Port and Bromborough. Integrated care boards (ICBs) are responsible fo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Eating Disorders: Ellesmere Port and Bromborough
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what estimate he has made of the number of patients referred to care outside the local area for patients with eating disorders in Ellesmere Port and Bromborough constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We do hold this data, however as it is below five at the current time we cannot provide an exact value. This is standard statistical disclosure control guidance, where counts of less than five are suppressed to stop individuals being identified.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Eating Disorders: Ellesmere Port and Bromborough
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the suitability of the provision of specialist eating disorder support for patients in Ellesmere Port and Bromborough constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department is working closely with NHS England to strengthen community-based eating disorder services and to improve outcomes and recovery for patients, including in Ellesmere Port and Bromborough. Integrated care boards (ICBs) are responsible for com...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Eating Disorders: Ellesmere Port and Bromborough
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to enable patients with eating disorders in Ellesmere Port and Bromborough constituency to access specialist services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Integrated care boards are responsible for commissioning mental health services for local populations. In Ellesmere Port and Bromborough, young people access specialist eating disorder services at Ancora House. A clinical assessment will determine which l...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Students: Loans
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she has considered taking additional steps to facilitate further Parliamentary scrutiny of amending the terms of student loan repayments administrated by Student Finance England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Parliamentary scrutiny is occurring in relation to the student loan system. For example, there has recently been a Westminster Hall Debate, as well as through the various mechanisms of parliamentary questions.It is worth remembering that these loans were ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

Trustee of Ellesmere Port and Neston Helping Hands, a charity which aims to provide poverty relief and support to residents in my constituency. This is an unpaid role. Date interest arose: 26 November 2020 (Registered 4 December 2020)
Member of Executive Committee of Tribune Group of Labour MPs. This is an unpaid role. Date interest arose: 4 November 2025 (Registered 3 February 2026)
